assessment.title: M6 The Polygraph and Writing Analysis Assessment

material.mattext: During the stress of lying, the body's involuntary actions are controlled by the
A large supply of blood is directed to the limbs during stress to
prevent the feeling of any pain
save energy that may be needed later
The stress responses that are monitored by the polygraph instrument are
blood pressure, digestive activities, and blood glucose levels
perspiration, heart rate, blood pressure, and breathing rate
heart rate, blood pressure, digestive activities, and pupil dilation
perspiration, heart rate, pupil dilation, and glycogen conversion
Select the four physiological activities below that occur when a person is telling a lie.
Increase in heart rate and blood pressure
Decrease in heart rate and blood pressure
Another name for the response that occurs during stress is
What did Leonarde Keeler add to the polygraph instrument developed by John Larson?
The first polygraph instrument developed by Dr. William Marston measured
What do the two pneumographs of the polygraph instrument measure?
Why is the galvanometer in the polygraph instrument able to measure perspiration levels using electricity?
Oils in sweat absorb some electric currents.
Water and salt in sweat conduct electricity.
Heat generated during perspiration conducts electricity.
Sodium in sweat creates a small electric current.
The device that records electrical conductivity by sending an electric current onto the skin is called a:
The device that takes a reading of a person's heart rate and blood pressure is called a:
The device that allows for the volume of air within its tubes to change during breathing is called a:
Which choice correctly identifies the three phases of a polygraph examination?
Stimulation test, Questioning procedure, Control Question Technique
Stimulation test, Post-test interview, Control Question Technique
Pre-test interview, Stimulation test, Questioning procedure
Pre-test interview, Questioning procedure, Post-test interview/analysis
The underlying goal of the pre-test interview is to
impress upon the subject that lying will be easily spotted
resolve any of the subject's anxieties and uncertainties
inform the subject of all of his or her legal rights
question the subject about his or her personal opinions and beliefs
What type of polygraph questioning technique is used in criminal investigations?
During a polygraph examination for a criminal investigation, it is important that the subject does not
know whether he or she is being asked control or relevant questions
identify any of the guilty suspects involved with the crime
demonstrate any painful or negative emotions
understand the difference between truth and deception
During the pre-test interview a suspect becomes involved in the creation of the
What type of questions focus upon behaviours that are similar in nature to the crime being investigated?
A polygraph examination is an effective criminal investigative tool used to
argue whether a suspect's alibi is accurate
understand a suspect's motive for committing the crime
question a suspect's knowledge of crime scene details
interrogate a suspect in the hope of getting a confession
Those trained to administer polygraph tests to criminal suspects are usually
The forensic analysis of handwriting is based upon the premise that
each person has a unique handwriting style
each person writes certain words the same way
each person's writing tends to change according to his or her mood
each person's writing tends to remain the same since childhood
The slope of handwritten text is the most noticeable when
Often, the letters written in a deliberately disguised way are
What technique can be used to help individualize the type of ink used in a questionable document?
